title: mentha aquatica f. lloydii
intro: Mentha aquatica f. lloydii, also known as Watermint or American Watermint, is a subspecies of the watermint plant (Mentha aquatica) native to North America. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly its high menthol content, which has anti-inflammatory and antiseptic qualities. The health benefits of Mentha aquatica f. lloydii include relief from digestive issues, reduced fever, and eased respiratory problems. Herbal preparations of this plant can be found in teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to alleviate symptoms of colds, coughs, and sore throats.
